title: Turn off automatic update creation with Gmail integration
excerpt: I have a simple automation set up that sends an outgoing message from Gmail every time a new item is created. The items come from a signup form, so the integration essentially just sends an automatic confirmation email t…

posts.author: ajwhitman (AJ W)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a simple automation set up that sends an outgoing message from Gmail every time a new item is created. The items come from a signup form, so the integration essentially just sends an automatic confirmation email to the person who signed up. We also use the last “question” of the form (ie, “Is there anything else you’d like to tell us?”) that feeds into the update field.

My problem is, since every outgoing confirmation email creates basically the same update on every pulse, it adds a lot of noise and makes it difficult to quickly and easily determine who actually filled out the useful field. I’d like to be able to see (easily, visually) who has filled out that last free-form question based on looking at which pulses have updates, but right now I have to look closely at which have 2 updates instead of 1. And because it takes that extra effort, I don’t always notice if a new signup has added details, and I’m missing potentially important information.

Is there a way to turn off and/or mass-delete these automatic updates about outgoing messages? It would make my job much easier and improve my performance!

posts.author: V.B.Music (Mike  Alducin)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @ajwhitman! I also use some integrations in Monday and i also have a board for CRM. We used to have a similar issue. Maybe a solution would be to create extra columns and “Notify when new item is created” automation. That way, when a new person signs up, the info would populate your columns such as “name” - “mail” - “message” and with the notify automation you can have a notification such as “Whitman has just sign up, his email is whitman@reallycoolperson.com and the message is - i need help.”

posts.author: ajwhitman (AJ W)
posts.cookedHtml: That might work for small numbers of signups, but it really doesn’t scale. We recently had almost a hundred signups in one weekend! I can’t handle looking at that many individual emails or notifications. What I need is a system where I can go into the board on my own time, scroll through all the many new signups all at once, and see at a glance who added notes to their form - without the noise of a hundred notifications.

posts.author: ajwhitman (AJ W)
posts.cookedHtml: Update: I worked around this problem with Zapier. Now I (blessedly) don’t get notified of every outgoing email, either in my inbox or in the item update field. (However, it does use more “Zaps” than it did Monday automation actions.)

posts.author: jennap (Jenna)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @ajwhitman

It is not possible to turn of the automatic creation of updates that contain the information of the email just sent if you are using any of our internal email integration recipes.

However, I’m glad you were able to find a workaround using Zapier!

Another possible option for anyone who would prefer not to use Zapier or would like to also Filter Their Board is to use The Long Text Column for your final question (“Is there anything else you’d like to tell us?”):

image1710×920 72.5 KB

I hope this helps! Remember to mark as solved if this answers your question

Cheers,
Jenna

posts.author: mlh (Megan Hochstrasser)
posts.cookedHtml: This is insanely frustrating! I don’t want these updates and don’t see why they’d be enabled by default and impossible to disable. Any progress on this since 2021?

posts.author: AnyaP (Anya P)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree. It seems like there should be a toggle for this, depending on whether you want the automation logged in Updates or not…

posts.author: aw821 (Alicia Williams)
posts.cookedHtml:
There are ways to shut off these notifications if they are landing in your email box. It took me FOREVER to find it. I was getting duplicate emails from Monday and it was so much to sift through. I am not sure if this is what you are looking for, but there are MULTIPLE layers of notifications in Monday and you need to know how to turn them off.
This is ON the board itself. Then you have the notification under your user profile you need to tweak.

HTH

Screen Shot 2023-02-17 at 2.02.24 PM1792×1426 220 KB

posts.author: nomadicenergy (Charlie Low)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi AJ, would you be able to share how you set up the work around in Zapier? We are having the same issue, we have automations which rely on updates, but they are now getting false positives because of the update from the Gmail integration. No idea why you can’t just turn it off within Monday.

posts.author: mitchellgp (Mitchell)
posts.cookedHtml: Any progress with this one? This is becoming a serious issue for our team, just so many “outgoing email” updates on items

posts.author: Mayon85 (Marion)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

In my case, my automation sends an email to all board subscribers. So far so good. However, an update is created for each email sent. So I can easily have 5, 10 or even 15, unnecessary updates…
Things get complicated when I want to move my element to another board. I have to delete all the updates so as not to pollute the new board in which the element is newley located.

And to top it all off, I’m the owner of the automation, so I’m the only one who can delete the updates! Nonsense… please let us disable the automatic updates!

posts.author: BenBriggs (Ben Briggs)
posts.cookedHtml: I have a similar concern with Outlook integration. I have a simple automation that sends an Item owner a reminder email if a review date has been passed. This reminder email generates an unnecessary Update entry that is annoying to the item owners. Is there any progress on being able to suppress the automatic creation of an Update entry each time the automation fires? It has been 3 years since this issue was first posted concerning this very reasonable feature request. -thanks

posts.author: bmatevski (Bojan Matevski)
posts.cookedHtml: It is frustrating that no one from the devs or support is even replying to this thread. This is a serious problem. You can’t send an automated email without annoying people assigned to that item.
